full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic payload deck for the 2026 RobotX WAM-V 16 USV, compliant with the 2026 RobotX Team Handbook. The WAM-V has two 5 m pontoons connected by a 4 m x 2 m deck bridging structure on top of which the team must mount sensors, a UAV launch/recovery interface, and a UUV deployment gantry. Payload mass limit 500 kg on the deck. The deck is a honeycomb sandwich composite panel: outer skin 3 mm carbon/epoxy quasi-isotropic, 25 mm Nomex honeycomb core, inner skin 3 mm carbon/epoxy. Overall dimensions 4000 mm x 2000 mm x 31 mm. Deck attaches to the WAM-V inflatable pontoon rigid inserts via 8 clamping strap interfaces on each long edge (16 total) - model as pinned. Material: carbon/epoxy (E1 = 135 GPa, E2 = 10 GPa, strength L = 1500 MPa, density 1600 kg/m^3); Nomex honeycomb (density 48 kg/m^3, out-of-plane compressive 2.4 MPa, shear 1.2 MPa). Skin layup [0/45/90/-45]s. Load cases: (R1) Distributed payload 500 kg x 9.81 = 4,905 N uniformly over deck center 2000 mm x 1000 mm area; max fiber stress <= 1500 / 3.0 = 500 MPa; max core shear <= 1.2 MPa; max deck deflection <= L/200 = 20 mm. (R2) Concentrated UAV launch load: 80 kg UAV + 3 g launch recoil = 2,354 N at a single 500 mm x 500 mm pad location near deck center; max fiber stress <= 500 MPa; max deflection at load <= 15 mm. (R3) UUV deployment: 25 kg UUV hoisted and swung, producing 50 kg equivalent dynamic load at the deployment boom rooted at deck edge => 490 N vertical + 200 N horizontal; max fiber stress <= 500 MPa. (R4) Wave-hogging asymmetric load: full 500 kg concentrated at one corner (worst case of waves lifting one pontoon), 4,905 N corner load; max fiber stress <= 500 MPa; max corner deflection <= 30 mm. (R5) Deck structural mass <= 60 kg. (R6) Deck first modal frequency pinned at 16 strap interfaces >= 15 Hz (decouple from wave excitation <= 1 Hz x 10 margin). Primary verification: laminate-composite structural FEA (linear static x4) + modal (R6). Vibration secondary via R6. Thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 deck panel, laminate schedule, strap-mount drawings, and a report listing deck mass in kg, max fiber stress per case, max deflection per case, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic carbon/epoxy honeycomb-sandwich payload deck for the 2026 RobotX WAM-V 16 USV, compliant with the 2026 RobotX Team Handbook. The deck bridges the two 5 m pontoons and carries sensors, a UAV launch/recovery pad, and a UUV deployment gantry; total deck payload capped at 500 kg. Skins are 3 mm carbon/epoxy quasi-isotropic ([0/45/90/-45]s) around a 25 mm Nomex honeycomb core. The deck attaches to the WAM-V pontoon rigid inserts via 16 clamping strap interfaces (8 on each long edge, modelled as pinned).

Envelope: 4000 x 2000 x 31 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 distributed payload: 500 kg (4,905 N) uniform over a central 2000 x 1000 mm area; deck deflection <= L/200 = 20 mm.
- LC2 concentrated UAV launch: 2,354 N (80 kg UAV x 3 g recoil) over a 500 x 500 mm pad near deck center; deflection <= 15 mm.
- LC3 UUV deployment: 490 N vertical + 200 N horizontal at a deck-edge boom root (25 kg UUV swung, 50 kg equivalent dynamic).
- LC4 wave-hogging asymmetric: full 500 kg (4,905 N) concentrated at one corner; corner deflection <= 30 mm.
- LC5 modal: pinned at the 16 strap interfaces, first frequency >= 15 Hz (10 x margin over ~1 Hz wave excitation).

prompt.title: RobotX 2026 WAM-V payload deck
prompt.brief: Carbon/Nomex sandwich deck 4 x 2 m for 500 kg payload + UAV launch + UUV deployment loads.

boundary_conditions.mounting: 16 strap interfaces pinned (8 per long edge)
boundary_conditions.loads: distributed or point on deck surface

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Wave excitation out of scope at deck level.
